expectations.text: The agent fetches inline review comments via the REST API (gh api repos/.../pulls/.../comments)
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows the exact command: `gh api repos/whatifwedigdeeper/agent-skills/pulls/33/comments --paginate --jq '...'` — matches the expected pattern.

expectations.text: The agent fetches thread resolution state via GraphQL to filter out already-resolved threads
expectations.evidence: Step 3 issues a GraphQL query requesting `isResolved` and `isOutdated` per thread. The output shows thread #1004 (databaseId 1004) is `isResolved: true`, and the agent explicitly discards it: 'Thread for comment #1004 ... is already resolved — discarded.'

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept/implement/decline/skip before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Step 6 shows a markdown table labeling each comment with an action (fix / accept suggestion) and ends with 'Proceed?' — presented before any file edits or commits in Steps 7–9.

expectations.text: The agent waits for user confirmation before proceeding with changes
expectations.evidence: Step 6 ends with 'Proceed?' and records 'User response: Yes, go ahead.' before any changes are applied. metrics.json also records `confirmation_waited: true`.

expectations.text: Implemented changes are committed with Co-authored-by credit to the reviewer(s)
expectations.evidence: Step 9 shows the commit message containing `Co-authored-by: alice <alice@users.noreply.github.com>` and `Co-authored-by: bob <bob@users.noreply.github.com>`, crediting both reviewers whose comments were addressed.

expectations.text: Declined comments receive a reply via gh api with the POST method to the replies endpoint
expectations.evidence: No comments were declined in this run. Step 10 ('Reply to Declined Comments') is explicitly skipped: 'No comments were declined in this run. Step skipped.' The code path for decline replies is never exercised, so this expectation cannot be verified.

expectations.text: Resolved threads are marked via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation
expectations.evidence: Step 11 shows three separate `resolveReviewThread` mutations for thread IDs ThreadAAA, ThreadBBB, and ThreadCCC, each returning `isResolved: true`. The already-resolved ThreadDDD is correctly left untouched.

expectations.text: The agent fetches review comments and thread state before making any decisions
expectations.evidence: Step 2 fetches inline review comments via REST API and Step 3 fetches thread resolution state via GraphQL. Both occur before Step 5 where decisions are made.

expectations.text: The plan presented to the user includes a decline section with a reason for the out-of-scope comment
expectations.evidence: Step 6 table shows comment #3003 with action 'decline' and note 'Out of scope for this PR; a full OAuth2 migration is a separate project-level decision'.

expectations.text: The agent posts a reply to the declined comment explaining it is out of scope
expectations.evidence: Step 10 posts a reply to comment #3003 via gh api with body explaining: 'Migrating to OAuth2 is a meaningful improvement, but it's a substantial architectural change that goes beyond the scope of this PR...'

expectations.text: Declined threads are NOT resolved (left open for reviewer to see the reply)
expectations.evidence: Step 11 explicitly states: 'Thread #3003 (alice / OAuth2 rewrite) is intentionally left unresolved — the reviewer can see the reply and respond.' Only threads 3001, 3002, and 3004 are resolved.

expectations.text: Addressed (implemented) threads ARE resolved via GraphQL resolveReviewThread
expectations.evidence: Step 11 resolves threads PRRT_kwDOABCD01, PRRT_kwDOABCD02, and PRRT_kwDOABCD04 (corresponding to comments 3001, 3002, 3004) using GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation, each returning isResolved: true.

expectations.text: In-scope comments are implemented and committed
expectations.evidence: Step 8 applies all three in-scope changes to src/session.py (null check, SESSION_TIMEOUT constant, typo fix). Step 9 commits with 'git add src/session.py' and 'git commit', resulting in commit abc1234.

expectations.text: The commit includes Co-authored-by trailers for each reviewer whose feedback was implemented
expectations.evidence: Step 9 commit message includes 'Co-authored-by: bob <bob@users.noreply.github.com>' and 'Co-authored-by: carol <carol@users.noreply.github.com>' — the two reviewers whose suggestions were implemented. @alice's suggestion was declined so she is correctly omitted.

expectations.text: The final report distinguishes between implemented, declined, and skipped items
expectations.evidence: Step 12 report explicitly states: 'Implemented 3 comments → committed abc1234 / Declined 1 comment → replied with explanation / Skipped 0 outdated threads' and lists each action with its category.

expectations.text: The agent skips threads that are already resolved (isResolved=true)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 maps thread #6005 to 'isResolved: true' and marks it 'already resolved → skip'. Step 11 confirms: 'Thread #6005 was already resolved — no action taken'. Step 12 summary lists 'Skipped 1 already-resolved thread'.

expectations.text: When two reviewers flagged the same issue, both get Co-authored-by credit in the commit (deduplicated — one entry each)
expectations.evidence: Step 9 commit message contains exactly three Co-authored-by trailers: alice, bob, carol — one each. The transcript notes: 'Co-authors are deduplicated — one entry each for @alice, @bob, and @carol (3 total). @alice and @bob both receive credit even though their comments pointed to the same line and were resolved by a single edit.'

expectations.text: The agent replies to the clarifying question comment without resolving that thread
expectations.evidence: Step 10 posts a reply via 'gh api repos/myorg/myrepo/pulls/85/comments/6004/replies'. Step 11 explicitly leaves thread #6004 unresolved: 'Thread #6004 left unresolved (clarifying question — reply posted but discussion may continue).' The resolve mutation is only called for threads 6001, 6002, and 6003.

expectations.text: The agent uses gh api to fetch thread resolution state before deciding on actions
expectations.evidence: Step 3 issues a 'gh api graphql' call requesting 'isResolved' and 'isOutdated' for all review threads before Step 5 (the decision/classification step). The GraphQL result is used to build the thread resolution mapping that drives subsequent actions.

expectations.text: The commit message lists which changes were suggested by which reviewers
expectations.evidence: Step 9 commit message body includes: '- Add type annotations to validate_input parameters (suggested by @alice, @bob)' and '- Replace loop with list comprehension for better performance (suggested by @carol)' — each change is attributed to the reviewer(s) who suggested it.

expectations.text: The agent passes the explicit PR number 47 to gh pr view
expectations.evidence: Step 1 shows: 'gh pr view 47 --json number,url,title,baseRefName,headRefName' — PR number 47 is passed directly as a positional argument.

expectations.text: The agent identifies comments containing suggestion fenced code blocks as suggested changes
expectations.evidence: Step 2 output notes: 'Comments #2001 and #2002 contain suggestion fenced blocks → flagged as suggested changes.' Step 5 categorizes both as 'accept suggestion' actions, distinct from plain-text comments #2003 and #2004.

expectations.text: Suggested changes are applied as local file edits (not via a batch API)
expectations.evidence: Step 7 shows Before/After diffs applied directly to src/batch.py as local edits with text like 'Applying suggestion from comment #2001 to src/batch.py lines 20' followed by explicit before/after code blocks. No batch API call is made.

expectations.text: The commit message includes bullet points describing each change with the reviewer's handle
expectations.evidence: Step 9 commit message: '- Add type hints and docstring to process_batch (suggested by @carol)' and '- Add early-return guard for empty items in run_batch (suggested by @dave)' — both bullet points name the reviewer's handle.

expectations.text: The agent checks out the PR's head branch if the current branch does not match
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'Current branch is main; PR head branch is feature/batch-processing — checkout required.' Then 'gh pr checkout 47' is executed, output: 'Switched to branch feature/batch-processing'.

expectations.text: Outdated threads (isOutdated=true) are skipped without posting a reply
expectations.evidence: Step 3 identifies comment #2003 with isOutdated=true. Step 5 Decision for #2003: 'skip (outdated thread, no reply, no resolution)'. Step 10 (Reply to Declined Comments) is empty. Step 11 notes 'Thread #2003 (outdated, skipped) — not resolved per skill policy.' No reply is posted.

expectations.text: Already-resolved threads are filtered out before presenting the plan
expectations.evidence: Step 3: Comment #2004 with isResolved=true is flagged as 'filtered out, no action needed.' In Step 6 plan table, it appears as a row but with action '—' and note 'resolved, no action' — clearly excluded from actionable items before the plan is presented to the user.

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept suggestion, implement, and decline before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Step 5 categorizes all four comments (#4001 → 'implement (fix)', #4002 → 'implement (fix)', #4003 → 'decline', #4004 → 'accept suggestion') and Step 6 presents a formatted table with actions. This occurs before any code changes are applied (Steps 7–8).

expectations.text: The agent uses the gh api GraphQL endpoint to fetch thread resolution state and node IDs
expectations.evidence: Step 3 issues a `gh api graphql` query with fields `id`, `isResolved`, `isOutdated`, and `comments(first:1) { nodes { databaseId } }`, retrieving the thread node IDs (e.g., `PRRT_kwDOABC123_thread_4001`) and resolution state for all four threads.

expectations.text: The agent applies the suggested change locally from the suggestion code block in the comment body
expectations.evidence: Step 7 explicitly extracts the suggestion from comment #4004's fenced `suggestion` block and applies it verbatim: changing `if item == None:` to `if item is None:` at line 55 of src/processor.py.

expectations.text: The agent commits with Co-authored-by trailers for the reviewers whose suggestions were implemented
expectations.evidence: Step 9 shows the git commit message includes `Co-authored-by: alice <alice@users.noreply.github.com>`, `Co-authored-by: bob <bob@users.noreply.github.com>`, and `Co-authored-by: dave <dave@users.noreply.github.com>` — one trailer per reviewer whose feedback was implemented or accepted.

expectations.text: The agent posts a reply to the declined out-of-scope comment explaining why it won't be done
expectations.evidence: Step 10 posts a reply to comment #4003 via `gh api repos/myorg/myrepo/pulls/62/comments/4003/replies` with body: 'Rewriting this module in Rust would be a significant architectural change and is out of scope for this PR... I'll open a follow-up issue to track the performance investigation.' HTTP 201 returned.

expectations.text: The agent calls the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation to resolve implemented threads
expectations.evidence: Step 11 calls `resolveReviewThread` mutations for threads #4001, #4002, and #4004, each returning `{"isResolved": true}`. These correspond to the three comments that were implemented or had suggestions accepted.

expectations.text: The agent does NOT resolve the declined comment's thread
expectations.evidence: Step 11 explicitly states 'Thread left open: #4003 (declined — awaiting reviewer acknowledgment)'. No `resolveReviewThread` mutation is called for `PRRT_kwDOABC123_thread_4003`. The final summary confirms it: 'Declined 1 comment → replied with explanation' with thread left open.

expectations.text: The agent skips outdated threads (isOutdated=true) without posting replies to them
expectations.evidence: Step 5 explicitly marks #5001 and #5004 as 'skip — thread is outdated'. Step 10 states 'No replies posted. (Outdated threads are skipped without reply per skill spec.)' metrics.json confirms outdated_replied: 0 and outdated_skipped: 2.

expectations.text: The agent distinguishes outdated threads from regular ones in the plan summary
expectations.evidence: Step 6 plan table explicitly labels rows 1 and 4 with action 'skip' and note 'outdated thread', while rows 2 and 3 have actions 'fix' and 'accept suggestion' with no outdated note. The distinction is clear and prominent.

expectations.text: The agent implements the valid error handling suggestion and commits the change
expectations.evidence: Step 8 shows the None-check implementation added to validate(): 'if data is None: raise ValueError("Input data must not be None")'. Step 9 shows a successful commit (a3f9c12) with message crediting @bob and noting 'Add None check in validate() to raise ValueError on None input'.

expectations.text: The agent applies the suggested change from the docstring suggestion block
expectations.evidence: Step 7 shows the suggestion from comment #5003 applied verbatim: docstring changed from '"""Parse the input."""' to '"""Parse the input data and return a structured dict."""'. The change is included in the same commit (a3f9c12).

expectations.text: The agent only resolves threads that were actually addressed, not the outdated ones
expectations.evidence: Step 11 resolves only thread PRRT_kwDOABCDEF2345 (#5002, bob) and PRRT_kwDOABCDEF3456 (#5003, carol). Explicitly states: 'Threads #5001 and #5004 (outdated, skipped) are not resolved — no action taken on them.' Skill behavior summary table confirms this.

expectations.text: The final report categorizes actions: implemented, skipped-outdated, etc.
expectations.evidence: Step 12 report clearly categorizes: 'Applied 1 suggestion + implemented 1 comment', 'Declined 0 comments', 'Skipped 2 outdated threads (no reply posted)'. The detailed breakdown labels each item: 'Accepted', 'Implemented', 'Skipped (outdated)' x2. The Skill Behavior Summary table at the end also provides categorical verification.

expectations.text: The agent fetches inline review comments via the REST API (gh api repos/.../pulls/.../comments)
expectations.evidence: After failing with 'gh pr comments' and 'gh pr review', the agent discovered and used the correct endpoint: 'gh api repos/whatifwedigdeeper/agent-skills/pulls/33/comments', which returned the four inline review comments.

expectations.text: The agent fetches thread resolution state via GraphQL to filter out already-resolved threads
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly notes: 'The raw REST API response does not include isResolved status. The agent cannot distinguish resolved vs. unresolved threads from this endpoint alone.' The agent never called 'gh api graphql' to check resolution state, and consequently treated the already-resolved comment #1004 the same as unresolved ones.

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept/implement/decline/skip before making any changes
expectations.evidence: The transcript's 'Summary of What Went Wrong' explicitly states: '5. No structured plan shown — the agent jumped straight into making edits without presenting a plan for the user to confirm.' metrics.json also confirms plan_shown: false.

expectations.text: The agent waits for user confirmation before proceeding with changes
expectations.evidence: The transcript states: '6. No confirmation before editing — the agent applied changes directly without pausing to ask "does this look right?"' metrics.json confirms confirmation_waited: false.

expectations.text: Implemented changes are committed with Co-authored-by credit to the reviewer(s)
expectations.evidence: The commit command shown in the transcript is 'git commit -m "Address PR review comments"' with no Co-authored-by trailer crediting alice, bob, or carol.

expectations.text: Declined comments receive a reply via gh api with the POST method to the replies endpoint
expectations.evidence: The transcript notes: 'The agent does not post any reply comments to the threads.' No comments were declined, and no POST requests to any replies endpoint were made at any point.

expectations.text: Resolved threads are marked via GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states: 'The agent does not attempt to resolve review threads. It has no awareness of the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation... The agent never calls gh api graphql with the resolve mutation because it doesn't know that mechanism exists.'

expectations.text: The agent fetches review comments and thread state before making any decisions
expectations.evidence: The agent ran `gh pr view 51 --json title,body,comments` which fetches PR comments, not review threads. It did not query the review threads API (e.g. `gh api repos/.../pulls/51/comments` or GraphQL `reviewThreads`) to obtain thread state (isResolved, thread IDs). The transcript self-acknowledges this is without skill guidance and never retrieves thread state.

expectations.text: The plan presented to the user includes a decline section with a reason for the out-of-scope comment
expectations.evidence: The agent jumped straight into making changes with no upfront plan shown to the user. The transcript itself notes under 'What I would NOT do': 'No structured classification plan shown upfront — I jumped straight into doing things without presenting a plan (accept/decline/defer table) for user confirmation.' While a summary table appears at the end, it is a retrospective gap analysis, not a pre-execution plan presented for user confirmation.

expectations.text: The agent posts a reply to the declined comment explaining it is out of scope
expectations.evidence: The agent does post a reply to @alice's comment (#3003): `gh pr comment 51 --body "Thanks @alice — rewriting the auth module to OAuth2 is a great idea but out of scope for this PR. I'll open a separate issue to track it."` This explains the decline reason. However, note the reply uses the wrong endpoint (top-level PR comment, not a threaded reply).

expectations.text: Declined threads are NOT resolved (left open for reviewer to see the reply)
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states 'No thread resolution — I never called resolveReviewThread via the GitHub GraphQL API to mark addressed threads as resolved. The threads stay open.' Since no threads are resolved at all, declined threads are also not resolved, satisfying this expectation — though for the wrong reason (it's a general failure, not intentional handling of the decline case).

expectations.text: Addressed (implemented) threads ARE resolved via GraphQL resolveReviewThread
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states under 'What I would NOT do': 'No thread resolution — I never called resolveReviewThread via the GitHub GraphQL API to mark addressed threads as resolved. The threads stay open.' No GraphQL mutation was invoked for any thread.

expectations.text: In-scope comments are implemented and committed
expectations.evidence: The agent showed pseudocode for changes to #3001, #3002, and #3004 but never actually ran any file edits or git commands. No `git commit` appears in the transcript. The changes were described in markdown code blocks only, not applied to any file. The metrics.json shows only 1 tool call (`gh pr view`), confirming no actual file edits or commits occurred.

expectations.text: The commit includes Co-authored-by trailers for each reviewer whose feedback was implemented
expectations.evidence: No commit was made (see above). The transcript explicitly notes 'No Co-authored-by in commits — Commits fixing the issues would lack Co-authored-by: attribution for the comment authors' as a known gap.

expectations.text: The final report distinguishes between implemented, declined, and skipped items
expectations.evidence: The transcript ends with a 'Summary of Gaps' table that lists failure modes, not a structured report of what was implemented vs. declined vs. skipped for this run. No items were actually implemented (no real code changes or commits were made), so the table cannot represent a true final status report. The gap table is meta-commentary about the without-skill run, not a task completion report.

expectations.text: The agent skips threads that are already resolved (isResolved=true)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 explicitly states: 'The agent would likely fetch comments but would NOT use GraphQL to check isResolved state. The REST API for pull request review comments does not surface the isResolved field.' Step 6 further notes the agent 'might try to re-resolve it or make an unnecessary comment on an already-closed thread.' The summary table confirms: 'Skip already-resolved thread (#6005) | Yes | No — may act on it unnecessarily.'

expectations.text: When two reviewers flagged the same issue, both get Co-authored-by credit in the commit (deduplicated — one entry each)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 states: 'The agent would NOT add Co-authored-by trailers for @alice, @bob, and @carol. Without skill guidance specifying this convention, the agent has no reason to include reviewer attribution in the commit message. The co-author count would be 0.' metrics.json confirms coauthor_count: 0.

expectations.text: The agent replies to the clarifying question comment without resolving that thread
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5 states: 'Without skill guidance, the agent has no rule distinguishing answer and leave open from answer and resolve. There is roughly a 50/50 chance it resolves the thread when it shouldn't.' No evidence of correct behavior; the transcript predicts inconsistent handling with likely incorrect resolution of the thread.

expectations.text: The agent uses gh api to fetch thread resolution state before deciding on actions
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 explicitly states: 'The agent would likely fetch comments but would NOT use GraphQL to check isResolved state.' The summary table confirms: 'Check resolved state via GraphQL | Yes | No — uses REST API, misses isResolved.'

expectations.text: The commit message lists which changes were suggested by which reviewers
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 shows the expected commit message as: 'Address PR review comments: add type annotations, use list comprehension' — a generic message with no per-reviewer attribution. No Co-authored-by trailers (coauthor_count: 0 per metrics.json), and no mention of which reviewer suggested which change.

expectations.text: The agent passes the explicit PR number 47 to gh pr view
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 shows: `gh pr view 47 --repo myorg/myrepo --comments` — PR number 47 is explicitly passed.

expectations.text: The agent identifies comments containing suggestion fenced code blocks as suggested changes
expectations.evidence: The transcript uses hedging language: 'The agent identifies the suggestion fenced blocks in comments #2001 and #2002 and decides to apply them.' However, the summary table marks this as 'PARTIAL — may work if body is visible', indicating this is not reliably done. The transcript also notes the REST endpoint output does not surface raw markdown fences clearly, making reliable identification uncertain. No clear evidence of consistent identification.

expectations.text: Suggested changes are applied as local file edits (not via a batch API)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 and 4 describe: 'Agent directly edits src/batch.py using Read + Edit tools' — local file edits are the described mechanism, not a batch API.

expectations.text: The commit message includes bullet points describing each change with the reviewer's handle
expectations.evidence: Step 7 states the commit message is generic ('Address review comments on PR #47') and explicitly notes 'Does not add Co-authored-by: trailers for carol/dave (whose suggestions were applied)'. No bullet points or reviewer handles in the commit message.

expectations.text: The agent checks out the PR's head branch if the current branch does not match
expectations.evidence: Step 3 explicitly states: 'Miss the branch checkout step entirely. The agent is on main. It does not switch to feature/batch-processing before editing.' The summary table confirms: 'Check out correct branch before editing — MISSED — edits on main'.

expectations.text: Outdated threads (isOutdated=true) are skipped without posting a reply
expectations.evidence: Step 6 states: 'The agent has no signal from the REST API that these are outdated/resolved... it cannot make this determination correctly.' The summary table shows: 'Skip outdated thread #2003 — MISSED — may attempt to apply'. The agent uses REST (not GraphQL) and therefore has no isOutdated information.

expectations.text: Already-resolved threads are filtered out before presenting the plan
expectations.evidence: Step 6 states the agent cannot determine resolved status from the REST API, and the summary table confirms: 'Skip resolved thread #2004 — MISSED — may attempt to apply'. No plan presentation step occurs, and no filtering of resolved threads is described.

expectations.text: The agent presents a plan categorizing comments as accept suggestion, implement, and decline before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states '[No upfront plan shown to user]' and 'The agent immediately begins acting on comments without summarizing the plan, classifying comment types, or asking for confirmation.' Summary table also confirms 'Upfront plan shown to user: No' and 'Comment classification (valid/out-of-scope/suggestion): No'.

expectations.text: The agent uses the gh api GraphQL endpoint to fetch thread resolution state and node IDs
expectations.evidence: The agent fetches comments via REST endpoints only: 'gh pr view 62 --json comments,reviews' and 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/62/comments'. No GraphQL query for thread resolution state or node IDs appears anywhere in the transcript.

expectations.text: The agent applies the suggested change locally from the suggestion code block in the comment body
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states under Step 5: 'Critical miss: The agent does not recognize this as a GitHub suggested change block.' and 'The agent does NOT: Recognize the suggestion fenced block syntax and extract the replacement correctly / Apply the change as a local edit derived from the suggestion block content / Use the correct workflow (extract from block → apply locally → commit).' Summary table confirms 'Suggestion block recognized and applied correctly: No'.

expectations.text: The agent commits with Co-authored-by trailers for the reviewers whose suggestions were implemented
expectations.evidence: The commit is shown as 'git commit -m "Address PR review comments"' with no Co-authored-by trailers. Transcript notes '[No Co-Authored-By attribution in the reply]' for alice and bob, and explicitly states 'No co-authored-by trailer for the suggestion block commit'. Summary gap table also confirms this.

expectations.text: The agent posts a reply to the declined out-of-scope comment explaining why it won't be done
expectations.evidence: Step 4 shows the agent calls 'gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments/4003/replies -f body="This is out of scope for this PR. Rewriting in Rust would be a separate effort."' This is listed in the tool calls as step 9. While the transcript notes the behavior is inconsistent and lacks a conventional 'Declined:' marker, a reply explaining the out-of-scope nature is documented as the likely behavior and is present in the tool calls list.

expectations.text: The agent calls the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation to resolve implemented threads
expectations.evidence: Step 7 explicitly states: 'Critical miss: The agent does NOT resolve threads via the GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation.' The agent either stops after replying/pushing, attempts a non-existent REST endpoint, or leaves all threads unresolved. The summary table confirms 'GraphQL resolveReviewThread called: No'.

expectations.text: The agent does NOT resolve the declined comment's thread
expectations.evidence: Since the agent never calls resolveReviewThread at all (confirmed in Step 7: 'Leaves all four threads in unresolved state'), the declined comment's thread (#4003) is also not resolved. This is a degenerate pass — the thread is left unresolved because the agent fails to resolve any threads, not because it correctly distinguishes declined from implemented.

expectations.text: The agent skips outdated threads (isOutdated=true) without posting replies to them
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 8 shows the agent posts replies to all four threads including #5001 and #5004 (both outdated): 'gh api repos/owner/repo/pulls/71/comments/5001/replies -f body="Refactored the function..."' and similarly for #5004. The summary table confirms: '#5001 @alice: Agent Action = Made code changes + posted reply — Correct? NO'.

expectations.text: The agent distinguishes outdated threads from regular ones in the plan summary
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1 explains the agent used the REST API ('gh api repos/owner/repo/pulls/71/comments') which does not include isOutdated metadata. Step 2 states: 'Because the agent used the REST endpoint instead of the GraphQL reviewThreads query, it has no visibility into isOutdated: true on threads #5001 and #5004. It treats all four comments as equally valid.' The metrics.json confirms: 'plan_shown: false, outdated_handled_correctly: false'. No plan summary distinguishing outdated threads was produced.

expectations.text: The agent implements the valid error handling suggestion and commits the change
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4 shows the agent adds the None input check: 'def process(value): if value is None: raise ValueError("value cannot be None")'. Step 7 shows a commit: 'git commit -m "Address PR review comments from #71"'. The summary table confirms '#5002 @bob: Correct? YES'. The change was implemented and committed (though bundled with unnecessary changes from outdated threads).

expectations.text: The agent applies the suggested change from the docstring suggestion block
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5 shows the agent reads the suggestion block and applies the docstring change: 'It applies the change manually. Outcome is coincidentally correct.' The summary table marks #5003 as PARTIAL (applied manually by guessing rather than following a defined protocol, but the docstring change was in fact applied).

expectations.text: The agent only resolves threads that were actually addressed, not the outdated ones
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 9 states: 'The agent does not attempt to resolve any review threads via GraphQL mutations. It is not aware of the resolveReviewThread mutation or the workflow of resolving threads after addressing them. The threads remain open.' The agent resolved zero threads — it did not resolve even the valid ones (#5002, #5003).

expectations.text: The final report categorizes actions: implemented, skipped-outdated, etc.
expectations.evidence: No final report was produced by the agent. The summary table at the end of the transcript is part of the eval's own narration ('Summary of Failures'), not output from the agent. The transcript describes agent actions up through Step 9 (no resolution) with no mention of the agent generating a report categorizing its actions.
